Name: Meals on Wheels Victoria
Description: Hot meals delivered to your door every day by caring volunteers. You do not need to cook — just answer the door.
Eligibility: Seniors 60 and older who have difficulty cooking at home

Name: Better at Home Program
Description: Help at home for seniors — light cleaning grocery shopping and friendly visits so you can keep living independently.
Eligibility: Seniors 65 and older in Greater Victoria

Name: HandyDART
Description: Door-to-door bus rides for seniors and people with mobility challenges. Great for medical appointments shopping and activities.
Eligibility: People with disabilities or mobility challenges including seniors

Name: SAFER Rental Assistance
Description: Monthly rent help for low-income seniors so you can afford to stay in your home. Payments go directly to your landlord.
Eligibility: Low-income seniors 60 and older renting in BC

Name: Island Health Senior Clinics
Description: Medical care for seniors — checkups chronic disease management and referrals to specialists. Your health comes first.

Name: Adult Day Program
Description: Daytime programs with activities meals and friendly people. A great way to stay connected and enjoy your day.
Eligibility: Seniors who want social connection or need daytime care

Name: Community Meals Program
Description: Free hot lunch Monday to Friday in a friendly community setting. Come for the food — stay for the company.

Name: BC211 Helpline
Description: Call 2-1-1 any time — day or night — to find any service you need in BC. Friendly staff speak many languages.

Name: Home Care Services
Description: Care at home — help with bathing dressing and nursing visits so you can stay safely in your own home.
Eligibility: Eligible seniors assessed by Island Health

Name: BC Income Assistance
Description: Monthly money help for people with low income to cover basic living costs. Apply online or by phone.

Name: Senior Peer Support
Description: Volunteer phone calls and home visits for seniors who feel lonely or isolated. A friendly voice makes a big difference.

Name: Transportation Access Program
Description: Subsidized rides for seniors to medical appointments grocery stores and community events across Greater Victoria.
Eligibility: Seniors 65 and older in Greater Victoria

Name: Assisted Living Residences
Description: A home with support — meals housekeeping and personal care for seniors who want independence with a safety net.
Eligibility: Seniors who need daily support but not full nursing care

Name: Greater Victoria Food Bank
Description: Free food hampers for anyone who needs them. No questions asked — pick up weekly at a location near you.

Name: Mental Health Support Line
Description: Free 24/7 phone support for anyone feeling stressed anxious or overwhelmed. Counsellors are always available.

Name: Caregiver Support Program
Description: Resources education and rest time for people caring for a senior or family member. You deserve support too.
Eligibility: Caregivers of seniors or people with disabilities

Name: Seniors Legal Assistance
Description: Free legal advice for seniors dealing with housing issues elder abuse or money problems. Confidential and easy.

Name: Personal Emergency Alert
Description: A small button you wear that connects you to help the moment you press it — at home or anywhere you go.
Eligibility: Seniors living alone or with safety concerns

Name: BC Housing Registry
Description: Find affordable housing in Greater Victoria. Staff connect you with subsidized and supportive housing options.

Name: Recreation & Fitness Programs
Description: Fitness classes and social activities for seniors — aquafit yoga walking clubs and crafts. Something for everyone.

Name: HandyDART Booking
Description: Book your accessible door-to-door bus ride for medical appointments and errands. Easy to book by phone.

Name: Free Tax Help for Seniors
Description: Trained volunteers file your income tax for free — quickly and correctly. No cost ever. Bring your slips.
Organization: CRA Community Volunteer Income Tax Program

Name: Grocery Delivery for Seniors
Description: Free grocery delivery for seniors who cannot leave home. Nutritious food brought right to your door.

Name: Wheelchair and Walker Loan
Description: Borrow a wheelchair walker cane or other equipment for free — for as long as you need it.
Eligibility: Seniors and people with mobility challenges

Name: Senior Fitness Classes
Description: Gentle exercise classes for seniors — chair yoga light stretching and aquafit. Safe fun and social.

Name: Alzheimer Society Support
Description: Support groups information and help for people with dementia and their families. You are not alone.
Eligibility: People with dementia and their caregivers

Name: Dental Care for Seniors
Description: Affordable dental care for low-income seniors — checkups cleaning and necessary treatments at a reduced cost.

Name: Home Repair Program
Description: Free safety repairs and upgrades for seniors in their own homes — grab bars ramps and accessibility modifications.

Name: Volunteer Driver Program
Description: Free rides to medical appointments from trained volunteer drivers. They come to your door — no bus needed.

Name: Energy Bill Assistance
Description: Help paying electricity heating and utility bills for low-income seniors. Apply once and get ongoing support.

Name: Seniors Computer Classes
Description: Free classes for seniors to learn computers tablets and smartphones. Patient instructors make it easy and fun.
Eligibility: Adults and seniors of all experience levels

Name: SeniorsBC Digital Learning
Description: Free online guides and videos to help seniors use email internet banking and smartphones safely at home.

Name: Friendly Visitor Program
Description: Trained volunteers visit seniors at home for conversation and company. A warm connection for those who feel alone.

Name: Senior Abuse Support Line
Description: Confidential help for seniors experiencing abuse neglect or money exploitation. Your call is private and safe.
Eligibility: Seniors experiencing any form of abuse

Name: BC Transit Senior Bus Pass
Description: Reduced-price monthly bus pass for low-income seniors to travel anywhere on Greater Victoria transit.

Name: Respite Care Services
Description: Short-term care for seniors giving family caregivers a much-needed break — at home or in a care facility.
Eligibility: Seniors whose caregivers need temporary relief

Name: Adult Learning Centre
Description: Classes for seniors covering computers English writing and everyday life skills. Welcoming to all levels.
Eligibility: Adults and seniors of all ages

Name: Taxi Saver Program
Description: Subsidized taxi vouchers for seniors who cannot use regular transit — for medical appointments and essential errands.
Eligibility: Seniors with mobility limitations who cannot use regular transit

Name: Library Digital Drop-in Help
Description: Drop in to the library for one-on-one help with your phone tablet or computer. No appointment needed — ever.
Eligibility: Seniors and adults needing tech help

Name: BC Seniors Guide
Description: The official BC guide explaining all programs for seniors — written in plain clear language. Easy to read.
Eligibility: All BC seniors and their families
